texts: "Cubeshape Parity, as the name suggests, involves fixing parity errors on the Square-1 during the cubeshape step. The main advantage to this method is that checking for parity occurs during inspection, which isn't factored into your final time." - Brandon Lin

3-2-1 / Parallel Edges (Even).svgBottom: <svg viewBox="0 0 100 100" stroke-linejoin="round"><g transform="translate(0, 100) scale(1, -1)"><polygon points="50,50 43,75 31,68" fill="#fff"/><polygon points="24,75 31,68 43,75 40,85" fill="#f80"/><polygon points="50,50 31,68 15,59 24,43" fill="#fff"/><polygon points="2,62 15,59 31,68 24,75" fill="#f80"/><polygon points="2,62 15,59 24,43 15,40" fill="#0f0"/><polygon points="50,50 24,43 31,31" fill="#fff"/><polygon points="24,24 31,31 24,43 15,40" fill="#0f0"/><polygon points="50,50 31,31 40,15 56,24" fill="#fff"/><polygon points="37,2 40,15 31,31 24,24" fill="#0f0"/><polygon points="37,2 40,15 56,24 59,15" fill="#f00"/></g></svg>
